steps.title: Understand the context
steps.content:
The motorman is chafing in his box, stewing over “the drudging lot of the laboring man.” The sentence “He registered discontent” describes what he did with that discontent — the word must fit the idea of making an inner feeling visible or evident.
  • Choice A — 'enrolled': “Enrolled” means to sign up or join a group. One cannot “enroll” discontent. ✗
  • Choice B — 'valued': “Valued” means to appreciate or prize. Saying he “valued discontent” would mean he cherished it, which does not match the tone of the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — 'disregarded': “Disregarded” means to ignore. The sentence is describing the emotion he is expressing, not suppressing. ✗
  • Choice D — 'displayed': “Displayed” means to show or reveal. He is outwardly showing his discontent, which fits the physical, observable framing of the passage. ✓

steps.title: Confirm the fit
steps.content:
The passage builds a physical scene (craning necks, chafing in a box) and ends with the motorman making his inner feeling outwardly apparent. “Displayed” is the only choice that captures showing an emotion to the outside world.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
